PEOPLE.question: Does a written security strategy exist?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: A written security strategy exists.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: A written security strategy does not exist.
remediation: Draft a 1–2 page security strategy that defines objectives, scope, and priorities (people/process/technology) and store it in the approved policy repository.
Assign an executive owner (e.g., CISO/CTO) and a maintainer to review the strategy on a fixed cadence and record approval/version history.
Communicate the strategy to staff via an all-hands or intranet post and require acknowledgement for leaders and security-impacting roles.

PEOPLE.question: Are security policies signed by leadership?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: Security policies are signed by leadership.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Security policies are not signed by leadership.
remediation: Add an approval workflow so security policies cannot be published without an executive e-signature and a recorded approval date.
Publish a leadership “policy endorsement” memo that references the policy set and clarifies expectations for compliance.
Re-issue updated policies after any material change and require leadership to re-sign before they become effective.

PEOPLE.question: Can employees report a risk without fear?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: Employees can report a risk without fear.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Employees cannot report a risk without fear.
remediation: Implement a confidential reporting channel (alias/form/hotline) that allows anonymous submissions and is monitored by a defined owner.
Publish a non-retaliation statement for security reporting and include it in the code of conduct and manager training.
Track report outcomes (triage time, closure) and share quarterly metrics to reinforce that reporting is welcomed and acted on.

PEOPLE.question: Do we conduct an "after action report" for incidents?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: An "after action report" is conducted for incidents.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: An "after action report" is not conducted for incidents.
remediation: Create a standard after action report template (timeline, impact, root cause, corrective actions) and require it for all security incidents above a defined severity.
Hold a post-incident review meeting within 5 business days of closure and assign owners/dates for each corrective action.
Track AAR action items to completion in a ticketing system and verify fixes with follow-up testing or evidence.

PEOPLE.question: Are security goals linked to specific business kpis?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: Security goals are linked to specific business kpis.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Security goals are not linked to specific business kpis.
remediation: Identify 3–5 business kpis (e.g., uptime, customer trust, audit outcomes) and map specific security objectives to each KPI.
Define measurable security metrics (e.g., phishing report rate, patch SLA compliance) and set quarterly targets tied to the mapped kpis.
Review KPI/security goal performance in quarterly business reviews and adjust the security plan when kpis shift.

PEOPLE.question: Is there a formal process to resolve "security vs. speed" conflicts?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: A formal process exists to resolve "security vs. speed" conflicts.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: A formal process does not exist to resolve "security vs. speed" conflicts.
remediation: Define an escalation path for security vs. delivery conflicts (who decides, required inputs, acceptable risk criteria) and document it in a short playbook.
Create a lightweight risk-acceptance form that records the decision, compensating controls, owner, and expiration date.
Run a monthly review of accepted risks with leadership to confirm they are still valid and to retire temporary exceptions.

PEOPLE.question: Do teams have "security champions" embedded within them?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: Teams have "security champions" embedded within them.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Teams do not have "security champions" embedded within them.
remediation: Select and train one security champion per team (or per product area) with defined responsibilities and time allocation.
Provide champions with a checklist for design reviews, threat modeling prompts, and secure release criteria tailored to their team’s work.
Hold a recurring champion sync (monthly) to share issues, track remediation progress, and escalate blockers.

PEOPLE.question: Is there a formal program to upskill staff based on new threat trends?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: A formal program exists to upskill staff based on new threat trends.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: A formal program does not exist to upskill staff based on new threat trends.
remediation: Establish a quarterly “threat trend” review where security summarizes new tactics relevant to your industry and identifies required skill gaps.
Create role-based learning plans (modules + expected completion dates) and assign them via the LMS with completion tracking.
Validate upskilling by running targeted simulations (phishing, vishing, social engineering) tied to the new threats and reviewing results.

PEOPLE.question: Does every employee understand how their daily role protects the company vision?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: Every employee understands how their daily role protects the company vision.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Not every employee understands how their daily role protects the company vision.
remediation: Create role-based “security in my job” one-pagers for major job families (HR/finance/IT/dev) that explain their top risks and required behaviors.
Integrate those role briefs into onboarding and annual refresher training, and require a short knowledge check for completion.
Have managers review the role briefs with their teams during a quarterly staff meeting and capture attendance/acknowledgement.

PEOPLE.question: Is "security first" a top-3 cited value in internal culture surveys?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: "security first" is a top-3 cited value in internal culture surveys.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: "security first" is not a top-3 cited value in internal culture surveys.
remediation: Add a security culture question set to the next engagement survey (values, psychological safety, reporting comfort) and track trend over time.
Run a focused internal campaign (posters/intranet/short videos) highlighting “security first” behaviors and real examples from the org.
Set a leadership objective to sponsor at least one security initiative per quarter and report back outcomes to employees.

PEOPLE.question: Do project teams have the authority to "stop the line" for security risks?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: Project teams have the authority to "stop the line" for security risks.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Project teams do not have the authority to "stop the line" for security risks.
remediation: Define “stop-the-line” criteria (what risks qualify, who can invoke, expected next steps) and publish it in the SDLC/project handbook.
Implement a rapid triage process (same-day) so security can evaluate stop-the-line escalations and unblock teams with clear decisions.
Train PMs and engineering leads on using stop-the-line and measure usage/response time to ensure it’s practical, not punitive.

PEOPLE.question: Is the security roadmap updated quarterly based on workforce feedback?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: The security roadmap is updated quarterly based on workforce feedback.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: The security roadmap is not updated quarterly based on workforce feedback.
remediation: Collect structured workforce feedback quarterly (survey + open comments) specifically on security pain points, tooling gaps, and policy friction.
Update the security roadmap based on top feedback themes and publish a changelog showing what was added/removed and why.
Close the loop by announcing delivered roadmap items and measuring whether the original pain point improved.

PEOPLE.question: Do employees receive annual phishing training?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: Employees receive annual phishing training.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Employees do not receive annual phishing training.
remediation: Schedule annual phishing awareness training in the LMS with mandatory completion and automated reminders/escalations.
Run quarterly phishing simulations with varied lures and ensure results are captured by department and role.
Provide targeted follow-up training for repeat clickers and track improvement over the next two simulation cycles.

PEOPLE.question: Is there a policy for handling sensitive data?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: A policy exists for handling sensitive data.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: A policy does not exist for handling sensitive data.
remediation: Write a sensitive data handling policy that defines data types, approved storage locations, sharing rules, and minimum controls (encryption, access).
Embed the policy into daily workflows (templates, checklists, ticket fields) so staff must choose a data classification during work intake.
Audit compliance quarterly by sampling files/transactions and documenting corrective actions for policy violations.

PEOPLE.question: Are employees told not to use public Wi-Fi?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: Employees are told not to use public Wi-Fi.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Employees are not told not to use public Wi-Fi.
remediation: Publish a remote-work network standard that prohibits public wi‑fi without VPN and provides approved alternatives (hotspot, tethering).
Configure endpoint policies to auto-start VPN and block access to sensitive apps when VPN is disconnected on untrusted networks.
Train staff with a short micro-module showing common public wi‑fi risks and require acknowledgement during onboarding.

PEOPLE.question: Do IT admins get extra training?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: IT admins receive extra training.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: IT admins do not receive extra training.
remediation: Define an “elevated access” training requirement for admins (AD/m365/cloud/EDR) covering least privilege, logging, and break-glass procedures.
Require admins to complete hands-on labs (e.g., privilege escalation detection, secure configuration) and record completion in HR/LMS.
Perform annual admin re-certification and remove elevated access for accounts that do not meet training requirements.

PEOPLE.question: Does the board receive a security briefing?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: The board receives a security briefing.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: The board does not receive a security briefing.
remediation: Establish a quarterly board-level security briefing agenda (top risks, incidents, metrics, budget needs) and schedule it on the board calendar.
Provide a one-page risk dashboard with trends (phishing, vulnerabilities, third-party risk, IR readiness) and definitions in plain language.
Document board decisions and action items (funding, policy changes) and track them to closure like any other governance deliverable.

PEOPLE.question: Are monthly simulations tailored to specific department risks?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: Monthly simulations are tailored to specific department risks.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Monthly simulations are not tailored to specific department risks.
remediation: Segment simulation scenarios by department (e.g., invoice fraud for finance, candidate scams for HR, oauth app consent for IT) and plan a 12‑month calendar.
Use department-specific success metrics (reporting rate, time-to-report, click rate) and review results with each department leader.
Deliver short, targeted coaching to departments with higher risk signals and re-test with a similar scenario the following month.

PEOPLE.question: Is data labeling automated or prompted within the workflow?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: Data labeling is automated or prompted within the workflow.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Data labeling is not automated or prompted within the workflow.
remediation: Define a data classification schema and add labeling prompts into tools where data is created (email, docs, ticketing, file uploads).
Enable automated labeling rules (keywords, templates, destinations) for common sensitive data types and validate with sample tests.
Measure labeling accuracy via monthly sampling and tune prompts/rules based on mislabels and user feedback.

PEOPLE.question: Is MFA required for all remote access without exception?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: MFA is required for all remote access without exception.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: MFA is not required for all remote access without exception.
remediation: Inventory all remote access paths (VPN, SSO apps, admin portals, vendor tools) and enforce MFA on every entry point.
Block or retire legacy remote access methods that cannot support MFA and document approved exceptions with compensating controls.
Continuously monitor MFA coverage and alert on any account/app that falls out of compliance (new app, new tenant, misconfig).

PEOPLE.question: Are there specific training paths for HR, finance, and developers?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: Specific training paths exist for HR, finance, and developers.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Specific training paths do not exist for HR, finance, and developers.
remediation: Create role-based security curricula for HR, finance, and developers that focus on their top attack paths and required controls.
Assign the paths in the LMS with deadlines and require a role-specific assessment (quiz or practical exercise) to pass.
Review outcomes quarterly (score trends, incident types) and refresh modules where gaps persist.

PEOPLE.question: Does the C-suite participate in dedicated risk workshops?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: The C-suite participates in dedicated risk workshops.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: The C-suite does not participate in dedicated risk workshops.
remediation: Schedule semi-annual c‑suite risk workshops that review top enterprise risks, recent incidents, and the current risk register.
Run a decision-oriented tabletop scenario (ransomware, breach notification, business email compromise) tailored to executive responsibilities.
Capture executive action items (budget approvals, policy changes, risk acceptances) and track completion with due dates.

PEOPLE.question: Does the "report rate" significantly exceed the "click rate"?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: The "report rate" significantly exceeds the "click rate".
PEOPLE.no_attribute: The "report rate" does not significantly exceed the "click rate".
remediation: Instrument your phishing program to capture click rate, report rate, and time-to-report by department and campaign type.
Promote the reporting mechanism (phish button/alias) with a short campaign and make reporting frictionless on all devices.
Set improvement targets (e.g., report rate > click rate by x) and trigger additional coaching for groups that miss the target.

PEOPLE.question: Are "data mishaps" used as anonymous learning moments for the whole org?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: "data mishaps" are used as anonymous learning moments for the whole org.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: "data mishaps" are not used as anonymous learning moments for the whole org.
remediation: Create an anonymous “security lessons learned” channel (newsletter/intranet) where data mishaps are summarized without names or blame.
Standardize how mishaps are captured (what happened, what control failed, what to do differently) and review before publishing.
Track recurring mishap themes and convert them into policy updates, training snippets, or technical guardrails.

PEOPLE.question: Is passwordless authentication or hardware keys fully deployed?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: Passwordless authentication or hardware keys are fully deployed.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Passwordless authentication or hardware keys are not fully deployed.
remediation: Pilot passwordless or hardware keys with a high-risk group (admins, finance approvers) and document enrollment and recovery steps.
Roll out phishing-resistant authentication org-wide with a staged plan and clear cutover dates for legacy MFA methods.
Disable weaker authentication options once adoption thresholds are met and monitor for lockouts and support volume during transition.

PEOPLE.question: Is training verified through "live fire" drills or red-team exercises?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: Training is verified through "live fire" drills or red-team exercises.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Training is not verified through "live fire" drills or red-team exercises.
remediation: Define “live fire” objectives (who participates, what techniques are tested, success criteria) and schedule exercises at least annually.
Engage an internal red team or vetted third party to run the drills and require a written report with findings and evidence.
Convert findings into prioritized remediation tickets and re-test the same techniques to validate closure.

PEOPLE.question: Is executive compensation tied to the organization's security posture?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: Executive compensation is tied to the organization's security posture.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Executive compensation is not tied to the organization's security posture.
remediation: Define security posture metrics appropriate for exec incentives (e.g., critical vuln SLA, phishing resilience, audit findings closure rate).
Set baseline performance and agree on target thresholds with the compensation committee and executive leadership.
Review metrics quarterly with independent validation (security reporting) and adjust incentives when metrics are gamed or misleading.

PEOPLE.question: Do IT and OT teams meet annually?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: IT and OT teams meet annually.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: IT and OT teams do not meet annually.
remediation: Schedule a minimum annual joint meeting between IT and OT stakeholders with a defined agenda (asset changes, incidents, patch windows).
Maintain a shared contact list and escalation path for IT/OT issues discovered during operations or incidents.
Document decisions and assign action items (segmentation, monitoring, access changes) with owners and due dates.

PEOPLE.question: Is there a list of people to call during a hack?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: A list of people to call during a hack exists.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: A list of people to call during a hack does not exist.
remediation: Create an incident call tree that includes internal roles, key vendors, legal/PR, and after-hours contacts with primary/backup numbers.
Store the call list in an offline-accessible location (printed copy + break-glass file) and review it quarterly for accuracy.
Test the call tree during a tabletop exercise and measure time-to-reach critical roles; fix gaps immediately.

PEOPLE.question: Is there a written "clean desk" policy?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: A written "clean desk" policy exists.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: A written "clean desk" policy does not exist.
remediation: Write a clean desk policy that specifies what must be locked/shredded, when screens must be locked, and what areas are in scope.
Provide the necessary tools (lockable cabinets, shred bins, privacy screens) so employees can comply without workarounds.
Run periodic spot-checks (after-hours or random) and document findings and corrective actions with managers.

PEOPLE.question: Is there a policy for "behavioral flags"?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: A policy for "behavioral flags" exists.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: A policy for "behavioral flags" does not exist.
remediation: Define behavioral red flags relevant to insider risk (policy circumvention, unusual access requests, repeated security bypass attempts).
Train managers and HR on recognizing/reporting flags using a standardized intake form and escalation path.
Establish a cross-functional review process to triage flags, document outcomes, and ensure privacy/legal constraints are met.

PEOPLE.question: Is there a shared risk register for converged IT/OT assets?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: A shared risk register for converged IT/OT assets exists.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: A shared risk register for converged IT/OT assets does not exist.
remediation: Create a converged IT/OT asset inventory and add it as a scoped section of the enterprise risk register with shared ownership.
Define risk rating criteria for IT/OT items (safety impact, downtime impact, internet exposure) and standardize how entries are scored.
Review the converged register quarterly and require documented treatment plans for high-risk entries (mitigate, transfer, accept).

PEOPLE.question: Are roles/responsibilities documented in a formal RACI chart?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: Roles and responsibilities are documented in a formal RACI chart.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Roles and responsibilities are not documented in a formal RACI chart.
remediation: Draft a RACI for key security processes (access requests, incident response, patching, vendor reviews) and have leaders approve it.
Publish the RACI in a location accessible to all teams and link it inside the relevant process tickets/forms.
Validate the RACI quarterly by sampling recent tickets/incidents to confirm the documented roles matched actual execution.

PEOPLE.question: Are physical audits conducted and results reported?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: Physical audits are conducted and results are reported.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Physical audits are not conducted or results are not reported.
remediation: Define a physical audit checklist (doors, cameras, badge use, server rooms, shredding) and set an audit schedule by site.
Assign auditors and require evidence capture (photos, logs, sign-off) for each audit item and store results centrally.
Report findings to leadership with severity ratings and track remediation actions to closure with retest dates.

PEOPLE.question: Is there a cross-functional group (HR/legal/IT) to review insider risks?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: A cross-functional group (HR/legal/IT) exists to review insider risks.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: A cross-functional group (HR/legal/IT) does not exist to review insider risks.
remediation: Form an insider risk review group with named members from HR, legal, IT/sec, and define scope, cadence, and decision authority.
Create a standardized intake pipeline for insider risk leads (behavioral flags, access anomalies, policy violations) with required fields.
Document decisions and actions (access changes, coaching, investigation) and track follow-ups to ensure the risk is reduced.

PEOPLE.question: Are IT/OT security metrics integrated into a single glass pane?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: IT/OT security metrics are integrated into a single glass pane.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: IT/OT security metrics are not integrated into a single glass pane.
remediation: Identify a single set of IT/OT security metrics (vuln status, alerts, asset health, access changes) and define owners and data sources.
Build a unified dashboard (SIEM/BI) that consolidates IT and OT signals with consistent severity definitions.
Set alerting and review routines so the dashboard is used operationally (daily/weekly reviews) rather than as a static report.

PEOPLE.question: Does the team conduct quarterly table-top exercises with executives?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: The team conducts quarterly table-top exercises with executives.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: The team does not conduct quarterly table-top exercises with executives.
remediation: Schedule quarterly tabletop exercises that include executives and cover at least one high-impact scenario (ransomware, BEC, data breach).
Assign executive-specific roles (decision maker, comms lead, liaison to board) and rehearse approvals and communications.
Capture gaps as action items (policy, tooling, training) and verify fixes in the next tabletop cycle.

PEOPLE.question: Is the physical office designed to naturally prevent "shoulder surfing"?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: The physical office is designed to naturally prevent "shoulder surfing".
PEOPLE.no_attribute: The physical office is not designed to naturally prevent "shoulder surfing".
remediation: Assess workstation layouts for visibility risk and relocate screens away from public walkways or install privacy filters where needed.
Establish “screen lock” expectations and enable auto-lock timers via endpoint policy across all devices.
Add visual cues (signage, privacy zones) and include shoulder-surfing checks in periodic physical audits.

PEOPLE.question: Does AI/ML assist in identifying "burnout" or "disengagement" risk triggers?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: AI/ML assists in identifying "burnout" or "disengagement" risk triggers.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: AI/ML does not assist in identifying "burnout" or "disengagement" risk triggers.
remediation: Define what “burnout/disengagement risk signals” are acceptable to monitor (e.g., workload, after-hours alerts) with HR/legal approval.
Implement a privacy-respecting detection approach (aggregated trends, opt-in where required) and limit access to authorized roles.
Create a response playbook (manager check-in, workload adjustment, access review) so identified risks lead to concrete action.

PEOPLE.question: Are personnel trained and provided with awareness programs at least once a year?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: Personnel are trained and provided with awareness programs at least once a year.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Personnel are not trained or provided with awareness programs at least once a year.
remediation: Establish a yearly security awareness plan that includes required topics, delivery methods, and a completion deadline for all personnel.
Deliver the program through an LMS or tracked sessions and require completion evidence (attendance + quiz/acknowledgement).
Review training completion and incident trends quarterly to update the awareness plan for the next cycle.

PEOPLE.question: Is the training content refreshed quarterly to include real-world examples of recent internal or industry-specific threats?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: Training content is refreshed quarterly to include real-world examples of recent internal or industry-specific threats.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Training content is not refreshed quarterly to include real-world examples of recent internal or industry-specific threats.
remediation: Set a quarterly content refresh process that pulls examples from recent internal incidents, vendor advisories, and industry breaches.
Update training modules and simulations with new examples and retire outdated material to keep the program credible.
Validate relevance by surveying learners after each refresh and adjusting content based on low-scoring or confusing sections.

PEOPLE.question: Is training performance (e.g., quiz scores and simulation behavior) used to trigger "just-in-time" coaching for high-risk individuals?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: Training performance is used to trigger "just-in-time" coaching for high-risk individuals.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Training performance is not used to trigger "just-in-time" coaching for high-risk individuals.
remediation: Define “high-risk” thresholds (repeat phishing clicks, failed quizzes, risky access patterns) and document coaching triggers.
Implement just-in-time coaching (micro-training + manager notification) within 24–72 hours of the triggering behavior.
Track whether coached individuals improve in the next assessment/simulation and escalate to additional controls if not.

PEOPLE.question: Do your employment agreements incorporate provisions and/or terms in adherence to established information governance and security policies?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: Employment agreements incorporate provisions and terms in adherence to established information governance and security policies.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Employment agreements do not incorporate provisions or terms in adherence to established information governance and security policies.
remediation: Update employment/contractor agreements to include security obligations (acceptable use, confidentiality, data handling, reporting) aligned to current policies.
Require HR/vendor onboarding to confirm the security addendum is signed before system access is granted.
Store signed agreements in a system of record and perform periodic audits to ensure all active personnel have current versions.

PEOPLE.question: Are these agreements reviewed and updated annually to reflect changes in local laws, regulations, and industry best practices?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: Employment agreements are reviewed and updated annually to reflect changes in local laws, regulations, and industry best practices.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Employment agreements are not reviewed or updated annually to reflect changes in local laws, regulations, and industry best practices.
remediation: Create an annual legal/policy review calendar that includes employment agreements, security addendums, and jurisdictional requirements.
Assign owners (HR + legal) to track regulatory changes and document what agreement clauses were updated and why.
Re-issue updated agreement templates to recruiters and contracting workflows and retire old versions from circulation.

PEOPLE.question: Does the organization use an automated system to track and ensure that 100% of active personnel (including third parties) have signed the most current security addendums?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: The organization uses an automated system to track and ensure that 100% of active personnel have signed the most current security addendums.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: The organization does not use an automated system to track security addendum signatures.
remediation: Implement an automated tracking system (HRIS/CLM) that links each active person to the latest required security addendums.
Block account provisioning (or trigger an access hold) when the required addendum is missing or out of date for an individual.
Run a monthly compliance report for 100% signature coverage including third parties, and escalate exceptions to leadership.

PEOPLE.question: Are all employment candidates, contractors, and involved third parties subject to background verification prior to hire?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: All employment candidates, contractors, and involved third parties are subject to background verification prior to hire.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Not all employment candidates, contractors, or third parties are subject to background verification prior to hire.
remediation: Define which roles require background verification (employees, contractors, relevant third parties) and make checks mandatory pre-access.
Use a standardized screening vendor/process and document pass/fail criteria and required evidence retention.
Verify that no accounts are created or badges issued until background checks are completed and recorded.

PEOPLE.question: Is there a documented standard for the depth of background checks based on the sensitivity of the role (e.g., financial access vs. general staff)?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: A documented standard exists for the depth of background checks based on role sensitivity.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: A documented standard does not exist for the depth of background checks based on role sensitivity.
remediation: Create a documented tiered screening standard by role sensitivity (e.g., criminal check, credit check, education verification) with approval from HR/legal.
Embed the standard into recruiting and contracting workflows so the correct check level is selected automatically based on role classification.
Audit a sample of recent hires/contractors quarterly to confirm the screening depth matched the documented standard.

PEOPLE.question: Is there a "continuous monitoring" or periodic re-verification process for high-risk roles or long-tenured employees with elevated privileges?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: A "continuous monitoring" or periodic re-verification process exists for high-risk roles or long-tenured employees.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: A "continuous monitoring" or periodic re-verification process does not exist for high-risk roles or long-tenured employees.
remediation: Define which roles are “high-risk” (admins, finance approvers, privileged developers) and set re-screening intervals (e.g., annually).
Implement a periodic re-verification workflow with HR/legal oversight and document triggers for ad-hoc reviews (role change, elevated access).
Require documented completion before renewing elevated privileges, and record outcomes in the system of record.

PEOPLE.question: Are employees informed of their obligations for returning organizationally-owned assets upon termination?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: Employees are informed of their obligations for returning organizationally-owned assets upon termination.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Employees are not informed of their obligations for returning organizationally-owned assets upon termination.
remediation: Add an asset return clause to onboarding/offboarding checklists that lists device types and required return timelines.
Require managers and IT to confirm asset return (or shipment tracking) before final payroll/termination closure where legally allowed.
Maintain an inventory reconciliation report for offboarded users and escalate missing assets within 48 hours.

PEOPLE.question: Are all termination agreements reviewed specifically to cover post-employment confidentiality and intellectual property protections?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: All termination agreements are reviewed to cover post-employment confidentiality and intellectual property protections.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Termination agreements are not consistently reviewed to cover post-employment confidentiality and intellectual property protections.
remediation: Add a termination agreement review checklist that explicitly covers confidentiality survival clauses and IP ownership/return of materials.
Require legal sign-off for all terminations involving access to sensitive IP, customer data, or source code.
Store signed termination agreements centrally and verify completion during quarterly offboarding audits.

PEOPLE.question: Are documented policies, procedures, and guidelines in place to govern change in employment and/or termination?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: Documented policies, procedures, and guidelines are in place to govern change in employment and termination.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Documented policies, procedures, or guidelines are not in place to govern change in employment and termination.
remediation: Document end-to-end employment change/termination procedures (role change, leave, termination) including access updates and asset recovery.
Integrate the procedure into HR/IT ticketing with required tasks, owners, and SLAs (e.g., access removal within x hours).
Test the process quarterly by sampling recent changes/terminations and verifying evidence for each required step.

PEOPLE.question: Is a formal disciplinary or sanction policy established for employees who have violated security policies and procedures?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: A formal disciplinary or sanction policy is established for security policy violations.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: A formal disciplinary or sanction policy is not established for security policy violations.
remediation: Publish a disciplinary policy that defines security violations, investigation steps, and the range of consequences.
Train managers and HR on applying the policy and where to route security-related violations for consistent handling.
Log violations and outcomes in a tracked system and review quarterly to ensure consistency and identify recurring control gaps.

PEOPLE.question: Is there a clearly defined "escalation matrix" that ensures disciplinary actions are applied consistently across all departments and seniority levels?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: A clearly defined "escalation matrix" exists to ensure consistent disciplinary actions.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: A clearly defined "escalation matrix" does not exist to ensure consistent disciplinary actions.
remediation: Create an escalation matrix that maps violation types to required approvers and consequences (warnings, access removal, termination).
Require documented approval for deviations so exceptions are visible and not handled informally.
Review disciplinary actions quarterly across departments/seniority to confirm consistent application and adjust the matrix as needed.

PEOPLE.question: Does the organization perform a "root cause analysis" on policy violations to determine if the failure was a lack of intent, a lack of training, or a flawed process?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: The organization performs a "root cause analysis" on policy violations.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: The organization does not perform a "root cause analysis" on policy violations.
remediation: Establish an RCA process for policy violations that captures intent, contributing factors, and which control/training failed.
Assign corrective actions that match the RCA outcome (training fix, process change, technical guardrail) with owners and due dates.
Track repeat violations by category and verify that RCA-driven changes reduced recurrence over the next quarter.

PEOPLE.question: Are information security policies and procedures made available to all impacted personnel and business partners?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: Information security policies and procedures are made available to all impacted personnel and business partners.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Information security policies and procedures are not consistently available to all impacted personnel and business partners.
remediation: Publish security policies in a single accessible repository and provide business partners with controlled access where applicable.
Include policy access links in onboarding materials and vendor onboarding packages so impacted parties can find them quickly.
Require periodic re-acknowledgement (e.g., annually or upon major updates) and track acknowledgements for all impacted roles.

PEOPLE.question: Are these policies authorized by a specific accountable business role (e.g., CISO or HR director) and mapped to a recognized framework like ISO 27001 or SOC 2?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: Policies are authorized by an accountable role and mapped to recognized frameworks.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Policies are not authorized by an accountable role or are not mapped to recognized frameworks.
remediation: Assign a formal policy owner (role/title) for each policy and require their authorization before publication or major updates.
Map each policy to relevant control frameworks (e.g., ISO 27001/SOC 2) and document the mapping in the policy appendix or GRC tool.
Perform an annual framework alignment review to ensure policy statements still satisfy control requirements and update gaps.

PEOPLE.question: Is policy "readership" and "comprehension" tracked through interactive portals, ensuring employees don't just "scroll to the bottom" to click accept?
PEOPLE.yes_attribute: Policy readership and comprehension are tracked through interactive portals.
PEOPLE.no_attribute: Policy readership and comprehension are not tracked through interactive portals.
remediation: Deliver policies through an interactive portal that enforces reading checkpoints (short questions/confirmations) before acceptance.
Track comprehension with embedded micro-quizzes and require a passing score for high-risk roles before access is granted.
Analyze portal analytics (time-on-page, quiz misses) quarterly to identify unclear policy sections and rewrite them for clarity.

PROCESS.question: Are secure configuration baselines documented for network infrastructure?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: Secure configuration baselines are documented for network infrastructure.
PROCESS.no_attribute: Secure configuration baselines are not documented for network infrastructure.
remediation: Create a network device hardening baseline (e.g., CIS-aligned) that covers configs like AAA, management interfaces, SNMP, logging, NTP, and save it as a version-controlled standard.
Apply the baseline to routers/switches/firewalls using a standardized build template for new devices and a remediation runbook for existing devices.
Run a quarterly baseline compliance check (config diff against the standard) and track exceptions with documented risk acceptance and expiry.

PROCESS.question: Is there a documented process for granting and revoking user access?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: A documented process exists for granting and revoking user access.
PROCESS.no_attribute: A documented process does not exist for granting and revoking user access.
remediation: Implement an access request workflow that requires requestor justification, manager approval, and system owner approval for privileged roles.
Route all grants/revokes through a ticket or IAM workflow that records requester, approver, scope, and effective/expiration dates.
Perform a quarterly audit of granted access by role/system and remove permissions that do not match current job function.

PROCESS.question: Are security patches applied to operating systems and applications monthly?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: Security patches are applied to operating systems and applications monthly.
PROCESS.no_attribute: Security patches are not applied to operating systems and applications monthly.
remediation: Define patch SLAs by severity (e.g., critical within x days) and publish a monthly patch calendar that includes OS and key applications.
Use a centralized patch process (tooling or scripted deployments) to push updates and record deployment results per asset group.
Review patch compliance after each cycle and open remediation tickets for devices that miss the window, including compensating controls when needed.

PROCESS.question: Are critical system backups performed regularly?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: Critical system backups are performed regularly.
PROCESS.no_attribute: Critical system backups are not performed regularly.
remediation: Identify critical systems/data sets and define backup frequency and retention for each (daily/weekly, snapshots, database dumps, etc.).
Configure automated backups with monitoring so failed jobs generate alerts and the failure is triaged within a defined SLA.
Test restores on a scheduled cadence (at least quarterly) for each critical system and document outcomes, issues, and fixes.

PROCESS.question: Are these baselines applied automatically during deployment (e.g., infrastructure as code)?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: Configuration baselines are applied automatically during deployment via infrastructure as code.
PROCESS.no_attribute: Configuration baselines are not applied automatically during deployment.
remediation: Translate the network baseline into reusable automation artifacts (IaC modules, golden configs, or deployment templates) stored in a controlled repo.
Embed baseline enforcement into provisioning pipelines so new infrastructure cannot be deployed without passing baseline checks.
Add a pre-production compliance gate (policy-as-code check) that blocks deployments when baseline controls are missing or misconfigured.

PROCESS.question: Is access revocation integrated with HR systems for automatic triggers?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: Access revocation is integrated with HR systems for automatic triggers.
PROCESS.no_attribute: Access revocation is not integrated with HR systems for automatic triggers.
remediation: Integrate the HR system with IAM so termination and role-change events automatically generate deprovisioning tasks.
Configure automatic account disablement for key systems within a defined timeframe after HR status changes, with an exception workflow for legal/HR holds.
Reconcile HR rosters vs. active accounts weekly and escalate any mismatches until closed.

PROCESS.question: Is there a risk-based prioritization (e.g., CVSS score) for patching critical assets?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: A risk-based prioritization for patching critical assets exists.
PROCESS.no_attribute: A risk-based prioritization for patching critical assets does not exist.
remediation: Create a patch prioritization rubric that combines CVSS, exploit availability, asset criticality, and internet exposure into a single score.
Tag critical assets (crown jewels, externally exposed, privileged systems) and require accelerated patch windows for high-scoring vulnerabilities.
Hold a weekly vulnerability triage meeting to approve priorities, track exceptions, and verify completion with scan evidence.

PROCESS.question: Are backups stored off-site/offline and tested for integrity quarterly?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: Backups are stored off-site/offline and tested for integrity quarterly.
PROCESS.no_attribute: Backups are not consistently stored off-site/offline or tested for integrity quarterly.
remediation: Implement an offsite/offline backup strategy (immutable storage, offline media, or isolated account) for systems in scope.
Perform quarterly integrity validation (checksum verification, restore rehearsal) on offsite/offline copies and document results.
Define and enforce access controls so backup repositories are protected from ransomware-style credential compromise (separate admins/MFA).

PROCESS.question: Is there an automated "drift detection" system that alerts if a configuration changes?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: An automated "drift detection" system alerts when configurations change.
PROCESS.no_attribute: An automated "drift detection" system does not exist.
remediation: Select a configuration drift tool (network config manager or policy-as-code) and define the “expected state” for each device class.
Enable real-time or scheduled drift detection that alerts on unauthorized changes and links to the config diff and change ticket (if any).
Create a drift response playbook (validate change, rollback/approve, document) and measure time-to-contain unauthorized drift monthly.

PROCESS.question: Are access reviews conducted quarterly using automated "least-privilege" analytics?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: Access reviews are conducted quarterly using automated "least-privilege" analytics.
PROCESS.no_attribute: Access reviews are not conducted quarterly or do not use automated analytics.
remediation: Run automated access analytics to identify over-privileged users, unused entitlements, and privilege creep across key systems.
Conduct quarterly access recertifications where managers/system owners attest to access, with auto-removal of unapproved access by deadline.
Track least-privilege kpis (unused privilege removal rate, recert completion) and remediate teams that consistently miss targets.

PROCESS.question: Does the organization achieve a "zero-day" response time under 24 hours for critical flaws?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: The organization achieves a "zero-day" response time under 24 hours for critical flaws.
PROCESS.no_attribute: The organization does not achieve a "zero-day" response time under 24 hours for critical flaws.
remediation: Define a critical zero-day response playbook (intel intake, exposure triage, mitigations, patch testing, deployment) with 24-hour targets.
Establish an emergency change window and pre-approved compensating controls (WAF rules, EDR blocks, config hardening) for cases without patches.
Measure time-to-mitigate for each critical zero-day event and perform a post-event review to improve the playbook and tooling.

PROCESS.question: Can the organization demonstrate a full restoration within defined RTO/RPO objectives?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: The organization can demonstrate full restoration within defined RTO/RPO objectives.
PROCESS.no_attribute: The organization cannot demonstrate full restoration within defined RTO/RPO objectives.
remediation: Document RTO/RPO targets per critical service and map them to specific backup/replication methods and recovery runbooks.
Execute full restoration exercises (including dependencies and access) at least annually and partial restores quarterly to validate the plan.
Record actual restore times and data loss achieved, then adjust backups/architecture if RTO/RPO are not met.

PROCESS.question: Is there a master list of all active user and service accounts?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: A master list of all active user and service accounts exists.
PROCESS.no_attribute: A master list of all active user and service accounts does not exist.
remediation: Create a centralized account inventory that includes human users, service accounts, and API keys with owner, purpose, and system scope.
Require account creation to capture an accountable owner and an expiration/rotation plan for non-human accounts.
Review the inventory monthly for unknown/ownerless accounts and disable or remediate them within a defined SLA.

PROCESS.question: Is there a policy for the secure disposal of physical and digital media?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: A policy for the secure disposal of physical and digital media exists.
PROCESS.no_attribute: A policy for the secure disposal of physical and digital media does not exist.
remediation: Write a media disposal standard that specifies approved sanitization methods (wipe, degauss, shred) by media type and sensitivity level.
Use a controlled chain-of-custody process for retired assets, including secure storage before destruction and documented handoff to vendors.
Train IT/facilities on the disposal workflow and perform periodic spot checks to confirm the correct method is used every time.

PROCESS.question: Are logs collected from critical systems and stored securely?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: Logs are collected from critical systems and stored securely.
PROCESS.no_attribute: Logs are not collected from critical systems or are not stored securely.
remediation: Define which systems are ‘critical’ for logging and enable required log sources (auth, admin actions, security events, system changes).
Centralize log collection to a protected repository/SIEM with restricted access, tamper-evidence, and time synchronization.
Set retention and integrity requirements (e.g., write-once or immutable storage) and review log collection gaps monthly.

PROCESS.question: Are security requirements included in vendor and service provider contracts?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: Security requirements are included in vendor and service provider contracts.
PROCESS.no_attribute: Security requirements are not included in vendor and service provider contracts.
remediation: Add minimum security clauses to vendor contracts (MFA, encryption, incident notice timelines, subprocessor controls, right-to-audit).
Create a vendor onboarding checklist that requires security review before purchase approval for any vendor handling sensitive data.
Maintain a vendor register with data types shared and reassess security requirements whenever the vendor scope changes.

PROCESS.question: Is the inventory reconciled monthly to identify and disable dormant accounts?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: The account inventory is reconciled monthly to identify and disable dormant accounts.
PROCESS.no_attribute: The account inventory is not reconciled monthly for dormant accounts.
remediation: Define dormancy thresholds by account type (e.g., 30/60/90 days) and implement monthly reports for inactive accounts.
Disable dormant accounts automatically where possible, or open tickets to owners for verification and justification.
Track dormant account closure metrics and investigate recurring causes (stale service accounts, poor offboarding).

PROCESS.question: Are certificates of destruction obtained and logged for every retired asset?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: Certificates of destruction are obtained and logged for every retired asset.
PROCESS.no_attribute: Certificates of destruction are not consistently obtained or logged for retired assets.
remediation: Require a certificate of destruction (CoD) for each retired asset, including serial number, destruction method, and date.
Log CoDs in the asset management system and reconcile them against the list of disposed assets each month.
Escalate any missing CoDs immediately and suspend disposal vendors that cannot provide complete evidence.

PROCESS.question: Is there a centralized SIEM or log aggregator with 90+ days of retention?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: A centralized SIEM or log aggregator exists with 90+ days of retention.
PROCESS.no_attribute: A centralized SIEM or log aggregator does not exist or has less than 90 days of retention.
remediation: Deploy a centralized SIEM/log aggregator and onboard required log sources with standardized parsing and timestamps.
Configure retention policies to preserve logs for at least 90 days online (or searchable) and protect them with least-privilege access.
Run a monthly log coverage review to identify missing sources, ingestion failures, or parsing issues and remediate them.

PROCESS.question: Is there a formal process to validate vendor security (e.g., SOC 2 review) annually?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: A formal process exists to validate vendor security annually.
PROCESS.no_attribute: A formal process does not exist to validate vendor security annually.
remediation: Establish an annual vendor security validation process (questionnaire + SOC 2/ISO review) for vendors in scope.
Define acceptance criteria (control gaps, exceptions, remediation timelines) and require documented sign-off before renewal.
Track vendor findings as remediation items with due dates and follow up until closure or documented risk acceptance.

PROCESS.question: Is "just-in-time" (JIT) access used to eliminate permanent standing privileges?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: "just-in-time" (JIT) access is used to eliminate permanent standing privileges.
PROCESS.no_attribute: "just-in-time" (JIT) access is not used; permanent standing privileges exist.
remediation: Implement a JIT access solution for privileged roles that requires approval and grants time-bound access with automatic revocation.
Remove standing admin privileges from day-to-day accounts and enforce privileged actions through the JIT workflow.
Review JIT usage logs monthly to confirm privileges are expiring as expected and to detect abnormal access patterns.

PROCESS.question: Is the disposal process audited annually to ensure no data "leakage" in the supply chain?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: The asset disposal process is audited annually to prevent data leakage in the supply chain.
PROCESS.no_attribute: The asset disposal process is not audited annually for data leakage.
remediation: Audit the disposal supply chain annually (vendor sites/processes) to verify sanitization methods and chain-of-custody controls.
Sample retired assets at each stage (pickup, storage, destruction) to confirm no devices leave the process unsanitized.
Document audit findings and require corrective actions from vendors, including re-audit timelines for serious gaps.

PROCESS.question: Are logs analyzed in real-time by AI/ML to detect behavioral anomalies?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: Logs are analyzed in real-time by AI/ML to detect behavioral anomalies.
PROCESS.no_attribute: Logs are not analyzed in real-time by AI/ML for behavioral anomalies.
remediation: Define the anomaly use cases you need (impossible login patterns, privilege escalation, unusual data access) and map them to log sources.
Enable real-time analytics (UEBA/ML) in the SIEM and tune thresholds to reduce noise while preserving true positives.
Create an investigation workflow for anomalies (triage, enrichment, escalation) and measure false-positive rate and response time.

PROCESS.question: Do you utilize a "software bill of materials" (SBOM) to track 3rd-party component risks?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: A "software bill of materials" (SBOM) is used to track 3rd-party component risks.
PROCESS.no_attribute: A "software bill of materials" (SBOM) is not used to track 3rd-party component risks.
remediation: Require SBOMs for internally built and vendor-supplied software in scope, including component versions and provenance.
Store SBOMs centrally and link them to applications/services so vulnerabilities can be traced to impacted components quickly.
Integrate SBOM scanning into CI/CD or procurement to flag risky components (EOL libraries, known CVEs) before deployment/purchase.

PROCESS.question: Is there a written incident response plan (IRP) with contact info?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: A written incident response plan (IRP) with current contact information exists.
PROCESS.no_attribute: A written incident response plan (IRP) does not exist.
remediation: Write and publish an incident response plan that includes roles, escalation criteria, and a current contact list for primary/backup responders.
Create incident categories and severity levels with clear triggers for paging, executive notification, and external communications.
Train responders on the IRP and run at least one drill per year to validate the plan end-to-end.

PROCESS.question: Are the causes of security incidents documented after resolution?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: Causes of security incidents are documented after resolution.
PROCESS.no_attribute: Causes of security incidents are not consistently documented after resolution.
remediation: Adopt a post-incident documentation template that captures timeline, impact, root cause, and corrective actions for each incident.
Require an incident closeout review meeting to agree on root cause and approve remediation tasks with owners and due dates.
Track corrective actions to completion and verify fixes with evidence (tests, scans, configuration validation).

PROCESS.question: Is there a defined method for staff to report a security incident?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: A defined method exists for staff to report security incidents.
PROCESS.no_attribute: A defined method does not exist for staff to report security incidents.
remediation: Publish a single, simple reporting method (phish button, hotline, email alias, portal form) and ensure it’s accessible on all devices.
Define triage ownership and response SLAs so reported incidents are acknowledged and categorized quickly.
Train staff on what to report and run periodic reminders, then track reporting volume and time-to-triage.

PROCESS.question: Are table-top exercises (TTX) conducted annually with the response team?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: Table-top exercises (TTX) are conducted annually with the response team.
PROCESS.no_attribute: Table-top exercises (TTX) are not conducted annually with the response team.
remediation: Schedule annual tabletop exercises that cover high-impact scenarios (ransomware, BEC, data breach) and include key stakeholders.
Use a structured script with injects, decision points, and evidence requirements so the team practices real actions, not hypotheticals.
Document gaps and convert them into tracked improvements (playbooks, tooling, training) with deadlines before the next TTX.

PROCESS.question: Is a formal RCA performed for all "high" and "critical" severity incidents?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: A formal RCA is performed for all "high" and "critical" severity incidents.
PROCESS.no_attribute: A formal RCA is not performed for all "high" and "critical" severity incidents.
remediation: Define which events qualify as high/critical and require RCA, including minimum evidence (logs, timelines, contributing factors).
Run a formal RCA session for each qualifying incident and produce a written report with prioritized corrective actions.
Review RCA quality quarterly and ensure recurring causes are addressed with systemic fixes, not one-off patches.

PROCESS.question: Is there an out-of-band communication channel for use during a total network outage?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: An out-of-band communication channel is established for use during total network outages.
PROCESS.no_attribute: An out-of-band communication channel is not established for use during total network outages.
remediation: Establish an out-of-band comms plan (e.g., SMS/phone bridge, alternate email tenant, messaging app) and document when to switch.
Provision and test the OOB channel quarterly, including contact lists and access for critical responders.
Store OOB instructions offline (printed + break-glass doc) so they are usable during a full network outage.

PROCESS.question: Is the IRP updated within 30 days of a "post-incident review" to fix process gaps?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: The IRP is updated within 30 days of a "post-incident review" to fix process gaps.
PROCESS.no_attribute: The IRP is not consistently updated within 30 days of a "post-incident review".
remediation: Add a post-incident review step to the IR workflow that requires identifying process gaps and proposed changes to the IRP.
Update the IRP within 30 days of the review, including revised contacts, procedures, and decision criteria, and record version history.
Communicate IRP updates to responders and run a mini-drill on the changed sections to confirm understanding.

PROCESS.question: Are RCA findings fed back into the development and govern domains to prevent recurrence?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: RCA findings are fed back into the development and govern domains to prevent recurrence.
PROCESS.no_attribute: RCA findings are not fed back into other domains to prevent recurrence.
remediation: Create a feedback loop where RCA corrective actions are categorized and routed to the appropriate domain owners (dev, governance, IT ops).
Require each routed action to be tracked as a ticket/backlog item with priority, owner, and acceptance criteria.
Verify recurrence prevention by checking whether similar incidents decline after the changes and adjusting controls if not.

PROCESS.question: Is there a pre-approved "crisis communication" playbook for public/client notification?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: A pre-approved "crisis communication" playbook exists for public/client notification.
PROCESS.no_attribute: A pre-approved "crisis communication" playbook does not exist.
remediation: Draft a crisis communications playbook with pre-approved templates for customers, regulators, and internal updates by incident type.
Define the approval chain (legal/PR/exec) and establish a rapid review workflow for time-sensitive notifications.
Run an annual comms drill during a tabletop exercise and update templates based on what slowed decisions down.

PROCESS.question: Are all software and hardware changes authorized before implementation?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: All software and hardware changes are authorized before implementation.
PROCESS.no_attribute: Software and hardware changes are not consistently authorized before implementation.
remediation: Implement a change authorization policy that requires risk assessment and approval before any production change is executed.
Use a change ticketing system to record change scope, testing evidence, rollback plan, and approvers for each change.
Perform periodic change audits to confirm unauthorized changes are identified and addressed with corrective actions.

PROCESS.question: Are security requirements defined at the start of new internal projects?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: Security requirements are defined at the start of new internal projects.
PROCESS.no_attribute: Security requirements are not consistently defined at the start of new internal projects.
remediation: Add a security requirements section to project kickoff templates (data types, auth requirements, logging, threat model needs).
Require a security review at project initiation to confirm requirements are captured and assigned to deliverables/milestones.
Track security requirements as project tasks with owners and verify completion before go-live.

PROCESS.question: Is there a "stage" or "test" environment used to validate changes before production?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: A "stage" or "test" environment is used to validate changes before production.
PROCESS.no_attribute: A "stage" or "test" environment is not used to validate changes before production.
remediation: Maintain a dedicated staging/test environment that mirrors production configurations and access controls as closely as feasible.
Require all production-impacting changes to be validated in staging with documented test results before approval.
Periodically verify staging fidelity (config drift checks) so it remains a reliable validation environment.

PROCESS.question: Are security "check-gates" integrated into the project management lifecycle?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: Security "check-gates" are integrated into the project management lifecycle.
PROCESS.no_attribute: Security "check-gates" are not integrated into the project management lifecycle.
remediation: Define security check-gates in the PM lifecycle (design review, pre-release, go-live) and document required artifacts at each gate.
Integrate gates into the project tool (jira/asana) so releases cannot proceed without the gate tasks being completed.
Measure gate compliance and exceptions, and review missed gates with leadership to prevent repeat bypasses.

PROCESS.question: Is there an automated rollback capability for failed or insecure changes?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: An automated rollback capability exists for failed or insecure changes.
PROCESS.no_attribute: An automated rollback capability does not exist.
remediation: Define rollback requirements for changes (versioning, feature flags, infra revert) and document how rollback is executed per platform.
Automate rollback where possible (pipeline rollback, blue/green, canary) and test rollback procedures on a scheduled cadence.
Require post-change validation and trigger rollback automatically when health/security checks fail within predefined thresholds.

PROCESS.question: Is "security debt" tracked as a formal metric and prioritized in the development backlog?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: "security debt" is tracked as a formal metric and prioritized in the development backlog.
PROCESS.no_attribute: "security debt" is not tracked as a formal metric or prioritized in the backlog.
remediation: Define what counts as ‘security debt’ (known vulns, deferred controls, insecure patterns) and create standardized backlog labels.
Require teams to log security debt items with impact, risk rating, and a target remediation sprint/date.
Review security debt metrics monthly (age, count, criticality) and set reduction targets tied to release planning.

PROCESS.question: Is there a central "source of truth" (e.g., HR system or active directory) for all user identities?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: A central "source of truth" exists for all user identities.
PROCESS.no_attribute: A central "source of truth" does not exist for all user identities.
remediation: Designate a single authoritative identity source (HRIS/directory) and document which attributes are authoritative (status, department, manager).
Integrate downstream systems with the source of truth so account lifecycle events are driven by authoritative changes.
Run regular reconciliation to detect identity mismatches across systems and resolve them within a defined SLA.

PROCESS.question: Is there a formal process to correlate "orphaned accounts" (accounts with no matching HR record) and disable them within a set timeframe?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: A formal process exists to correlate and disable orphaned accounts within a set timeframe.
PROCESS.no_attribute: No formal process exists to correlate or disable orphaned accounts.
remediation: Define what constitutes an orphaned account and set a disablement SLA (e.g., disable within x days if no HR match).
Automate correlation checks between HR records and account directories and generate a weekly orphaned account report.
Require owners to validate exceptions in writing; otherwise disable accounts and document closure evidence.

PROCESS.question: Is "identity analytics" used to automatically flag high-risk behavior, such as "impossible travel" or unusual entitlement creeping?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: Identity analytics are used to automatically flag high-risk behaviors like impossible travel or entitlement creep.
PROCESS.no_attribute: Identity analytics are not used to flag high-risk behaviors.
remediation: Enable identity analytics/conditional access to detect high-risk patterns (impossible travel, atypical sign-in, entitlement creep).
Define response playbooks for each signal (step-up auth, session revoke, password reset, access review) with severity thresholds.
Review analytics outcomes monthly and tune policies based on false positives and emerging attack patterns.

PROCESS.question: Are critical security alerts (anti-malware, firewall) sent to a designated mailbox or person for review?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: Critical security alerts are routed to a designated mailbox or person for review.
PROCESS.no_attribute: Critical security alerts are not consistently routed to a designated reviewer.
remediation: Define which alerts are ‘critical’ and route them to a designated on-call mailbox/person with clear ownership and paging rules.
Create an alert review checklist (validate, contain, escalate, document) and require completion evidence for each alert.
Track missed alerts and response times weekly and adjust routing/coverage until SLAs are consistently met.

PROCESS.question: Is there a documented "SLA" (service level agreement) for triaging alerts based on severity (e.g., critical alerts reviewed within 1 hour)?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: A documented SLA exists for triaging alerts based on severity.
PROCESS.no_attribute: No documented SLA exists for triaging security alerts.
remediation: Document triage SLAs by severity (e.g., critical within 1 hour) and include definitions for what ‘triaged’ means (ack, scope, next step).
Configure monitoring tools to time-stamp alert receipt and first action so SLA performance is measurable.
Review SLA performance monthly and implement staffing/process changes when targets are missed.

PROCESS.question: Is "SOAR" (security orchestration, automation, and response) used to automatically enrich alerts with context and close low-level "false positives" without human intervention?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: SOAR is used to automatically enrich alerts and close low-level false positives.
PROCESS.no_attribute: SOAR is not used for automated alert enrichment or closure.
remediation: Identify SOAR use cases suitable for automation (enrichment, reputation checks, auto-close criteria) and define guardrails to avoid bad closures.
Integrate SOAR with SIEM/EDR/email security so playbooks can enrich alerts and auto-remediate low-risk events consistently.
Review automated closures weekly via sampling to confirm accuracy and adjust playbooks when false positives or misses occur.

PROCESS.question: Does the team subscribe to at least one reputable threat intelligence feed (e.g., CISA alerts or industry isacs)?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: The team subscribes to at least one reputable threat intelligence feed.
PROCESS.no_attribute: The team does not subscribe to a reputable threat intelligence feed.
remediation: Subscribe to at least one authoritative threat intel source (CISA, ISAC, vendor advisories) and define who owns ingestion and distribution.
Create a weekly intel review routine that extracts actionable items (iocs, ttps, patch notices) and routes them to the right teams.
Maintain an intel log that records what was received, what actions were taken, and when, so follow-through is auditable.

PROCESS.question: Is there a repeatable process to take "indicators of compromise" (ips, file hashes) from intel feeds and search for them within your own environment?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: A repeatable process exists to search for iocs within the environment.
PROCESS.no_attribute: No repeatable process exists to search for iocs within the environment.
remediation: Define a repeatable IOC hunting workflow (collect iocs, normalize format, run searches in SIEM/EDR/DNS/proxy) with documented steps.
Create a standard ticket template to record hunt scope, results, and any containment actions taken when matches are found.
Run IOC hunts on a scheduled cadence (e.g., weekly) and after major advisories, then track time-to-search and time-to-contain.

PROCESS.question: Are external threat feeds automatically ingested and used to update firewall rules, EDR block-lists, and DNS filters in real-time?
PROCESS.yes_attribute: External threat feeds are automatically ingested to update security controls in real-time.
PROCESS.no_attribute: External threat feeds are not automatically ingested or used for real-time updates.
remediation: Integrate threat feeds with enforcement points (firewalls, EDR, DNS) using an automated ingestion pipeline with validation and rollback.
Define allow/deny criteria and change controls for auto-updated blocklists to prevent accidental business impact.
Monitor feed-driven rule updates in real time and audit outcomes monthly (blocks, false positives) to refine the automation.

TECHNOLOGY.question: Are host-based firewalls and disk encryption (e.g., bitlocker/filevault) enabled on all end-user devices?
TECHNOLOGY.yes_attribute: Host-based firewalls and full-disk encryption are enabled on all end-user devices.
TECHNOLOGY.no_attribute: Host-based firewalls or full-disk encryption are not consistently enabled on all end-user devices.
remediation: Use endpoint management (intune/jamf/SCCM) to enforce host firewall enabled + approved rulesets, and verify enforcement with compliance reports.
Turn on full-disk encryption (bitlocker/filevault) via policy, escrow recovery keys to a secure directory/vault, and block access for non-encrypted devices.
Run a monthly device compliance export to identify endpoints missing firewall/encryption and remediate via automated policies or re-enrollment.

TECHNOLOGY.question: Is anti-malware software deployed and centrally managed with signature updates?
TECHNOLOGY.yes_attribute: Anti-malware software is deployed and centrally managed with active signature updates.
TECHNOLOGY.no_attribute: Anti-malware software is not centrally managed or lacks consistent signature updates.
remediation: Deploy a single approved anti-malware agent to all endpoints/servers via centralized management and block unmanaged devices from the network.
Configure automatic signature/engine updates and enable real-time protection, tamper protection, and cloud-delivered protection where available.
Create an alerting workflow for outdated signatures/disabled agents and auto-open tickets until devices return to compliant status.

TECHNOLOGY.question: Is there a technical tool to scan for missing patches across enterprise assets?
TECHNOLOGY.yes_attribute: A technical tool is used to scan for missing patches across enterprise assets.
TECHNOLOGY.no_attribute: No technical tool is currently used to scan for missing patches.
remediation: Select and deploy a vulnerability/patch scanning tool (e.g., tenable/qualys/defender vulnerability management) and onboard all asset classes (servers, workstations, cloud).
Schedule authenticated scans at least weekly and ensure scan credentials are managed securely and rotated on a defined cadence.
Establish a scan coverage dashboard (asset count vs. scanned) and remediate any blind spots (network segments, credentials, agents) within a defined SLA.

TECHNOLOGY.question: Is there a centralized management console to enforce OS hardening and disable unnecessary services?
TECHNOLOGY.yes_attribute: A centralized management console is used to enforce OS hardening and disable unnecessary services.
TECHNOLOGY.no_attribute: OS hardening and service disablement are not managed through a centralized console.
remediation: Standardize on a centralized endpoint management platform (e.g., intune/jamf/SCCM/GPO) to apply OS hardening profiles and service disablement baselines.
Create hardened configuration profiles for each OS family (windows/macos/linux) that disable unnecessary services and enforce secure settings (e.g., smbv1 off, local admin restrictions).
Continuously validate compliance using configuration reporting/drift detection and automatically remediate or quarantine devices that fall out of baseline.

TECHNOLOGY.question: Is behavior-based (EDR) software used to detect non-signature-based threats and fileless attacks?
TECHNOLOGY.yes_attribute: Behavior-based (EDR) software is used to detect non-signature-based and fileless attacks.
TECHNOLOGY.no_attribute: Behavior-based (EDR) software is not currently deployed.
remediation: Deploy an EDR platform (e.g., defender for endpoint, crowdstrike, sentinelone) to all endpoints and servers and verify agent health centrally.
Enable behavior-based detections (script monitoring, memory protection, credential theft, lolbins) and tune alert severities/notifications to your SOC workflow.
Create playbooks for top EDR alerts (isolate host, kill process, collect triage package) and test them monthly with controlled simulations.

TECHNOLOGY.question: Is OS and application patching fully automated for both internal and externally-facing assets?
TECHNOLOGY.yes_attribute: OS and application patching is fully automated for internal and externally-facing assets.
TECHNOLOGY.no_attribute: OS and application patching is not fully automated.
remediation: Implement automated patch deployment for OS and applications using a central tool (WSUS/intune/SCCM, JAMF, linux repos, third-party app patching) with staged rings.
Define separate patch policies for externally facing assets (accelerated windows + maintenance bypass) and enforce them through tagging or asset groups.
Continuously monitor patch job success/failure and auto-escalate devices that miss SLAs, including temporary compensating controls (WAF/EDR rules) when patches fail.

TECHNOLOGY.question: Are secure management protocols (e.g., HTTPS, SSH) required for all network traffic?
TECHNOLOGY.yes_attribute: Secure management protocols (HTTPS, SSH) are required for all network traffic.
TECHNOLOGY.no_attribute: Insecure management protocols are still permitted for network traffic.
remediation: Disable insecure management protocols (telnet, HTTP, FTP) across network devices and servers, and restrict management to HTTPS/SSH only.
Enforce strong ciphers and key exchange (e.g., sshv2 only, TLS 1.2/1.3) via centralized configuration templates and automated compliance checks.
Scan management interfaces monthly to confirm only approved protocols/ports are exposed and remediate any exceptions immediately.

TECHNOLOGY.question: Are firewalls and port-level access controls implemented on all network segments?
TECHNOLOGY.yes_attribute: Firewalls and port-level access controls are implemented on all network segments.
TECHNOLOGY.no_attribute: Firewalls or port-level access controls are missing on some network segments.
remediation: Review network segmentation and ensure each segment has firewalling/acls defining allowed inbound/outbound ports and protocols based on business need.
Implement and document rule-change controls (ticket + approval) for firewall/ACL modifications and require justification for every open port.
Run quarterly firewall rule reviews to remove unused rules and verify least-privilege network paths between segments.

TECHNOLOGY.question: Are DNS filtering services used to block known malicious domains?
TECHNOLOGY.yes_attribute: DNS filtering services are used to block known malicious domains.
TECHNOLOGY.no_attribute: DNS filtering services are not currently utilized.
remediation: Enable a DNS filtering solution (secure resolver, endpoint agent, or network appliance) and route all client DNS through it.
Block categories and threat feeds (malware, phishing, newly registered domains) and log all blocks for investigation.
Review DNS block logs weekly to identify infected hosts or risky user behavior and initiate follow-up remediation.

TECHNOLOGY.question: Is sensitive data in transit encrypted using strong, agile cryptographic suites?
TECHNOLOGY.yes_attribute: Sensitive data in transit is encrypted using strong, agile cryptographic suites.
TECHNOLOGY.no_attribute: Sensitive data in transit is not consistently encrypted or uses weak cryptographic suites.
remediation: Inventory all sensitive-data flows (apps, apis, admin sessions) and enforce TLS 1.2/1.3 with modern cipher suites for each endpoint.
Implement certificate lifecycle management (issuance, renewal, revocation) and automate renewals to prevent expired cert outages.
Continuously scan public endpoints for weak TLS settings and remediate findings with configuration hardening and regression testing.
